ddls:
CREATE TABLE "incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "date_occurred" TEXT /* Date when the incident occurred */,
  "location" TEXT /* Exact location of the incident */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the incident location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the incident location */,
  "title" TEXT /* Short title of the incident */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the incident */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category of the incident */,
  "incident_status" TEXT /* Current status of the incident */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the incident */,
  "reported_by" TEXT /* Name and contact information of the person who reported the incident */,
  "incident_type" TEXT /* Type of incident (crime or non-crime) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the incident category (e.g., Theft, Assault) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Parent category ID (for hierarchical relationships) */,
  "category_code" TEXT /* Standardized code for each category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_categories_parent_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "officers" (
  "officer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each officer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the officer */,
  "badge_number" TEXT /* Badge number assigned to the officer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the officer */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the officer belongs to */,
  "rank" TEXT /* Rank or position of the officer */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit or division of the officer */,
  "supervisor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the officer's supervisor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("officer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_officers_supervisor_id FOREIGN KEY ("supervisor_id") REFERENCES officers ("officer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response record */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident the officer responded to */,
  "officer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the officer who responded */,
  "response_time" TEXT /* Time taken to respond to the incident */,
  "action_taken" TEXT /* Actions taken by the officer during the response */,
  "response_status" TEXT /* Status of the officer's response */,
  "response_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the officer's response */,
  "officer_arrival_time" TEXT /* Time the officer arrived on the scene */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_officer_id FOREIGN KEY ("officer_id") REFERENCES officers ("officer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for system access */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., officer, analyst, admin) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the user's role */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken by the user */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action occurred */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, create, update, delete) */,
  "object_id" INTEGER /* ID of the object affected by the action */,
  "object_type" TEXT /* Type of object affected (e.g., incident, officer, user) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
